LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEES INSURANCE COMPANY AND GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, AS TO THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF ADEQUATELY ALLEGES OPPRESSION AND FRAUD TO SUPPORT ENTITLEMENT TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES BY THE ALLEGATIONS THAT DEFENDANTS ENGAGE IN A PRACTICE OF MAKING MISREPRESENTATIONS, WHICH THEY DID TO PLAINTIFF, TO FORCE THEIR POLICYHOLDERS TO PAY A DEDUCTIBLE WHEN THERE NO CONTRACTUAL OR OTHER LEGAL BASIS TO REQUIRE THEM TO DO SO. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEES INSURANCE COMPANY AND GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, AS TO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANTS ISSUED AN INSURANCE POLICY TO HER, BREACHED THE UNINSURED MOTORIST COVERAGE IN THE POLICY, AND BREACHED THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING AND VIOLATED THE UNFAIR COMPETITION LAW BY MAKING MISREPRESENTATIONS THAT WERE DESIGNED TO FORCE PLAINTIFF AND OTHERS TO PAY A DEDUCTIBLE WHEN THERE IS NO CONTRACTUAL OR OTHER LEGAL BASIS TO REQUIRE THEM TO DO SO. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-27-2018 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2017 Continued To Sep-10-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Seth Safier, Esq. (415-639-9090 x102) of Gutride Safier LLP for Plaintiff.
John Moura, Esq. (213-615-7035) of Freeman Mathis & Gary for Defendant.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Sarah Huard Leffert's Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED in part. All depositions noticed by Ms. Leffert to take place in San Diego shall occur at the office of Ms. Leffert's counsel in San Francisco on mutually agreeable dates unless counsel for defendant GEICO agrees to and does pay counsel for Ms. Leffert $1200. If that counsel for GEICO agrees to make the payment prior to the hearing on October 30 and makes the payment by November 13, 2017, then all the depositions will take place in San Diego on mutually agreeable dates. Prior to the hearing, counsel are required to meet and confer in person at the courthouse or elsewhere to agree on mutually convenient dates. Counsel's inability to reach an agreement obviating the need for this motion is extremely troubling. Cancellation of previously scheduled depositions due to an attorney's personal concerns is appropriate, yet any costs incurred or to be incurred by the deposing attorney due to the cancellation should be borne by the cancelling attorney. This is basic civil behavior expected of all counsel and it is hard to understand why the parties did not follow it. Counsel for Ms. Leffert's estimate of the future costs that her client will incur due to the cancellation is reasonable and should be paid by counsel for GEICO, yet case law prohibits this court from imposing that payment. However, this court has authority to change the statutory location of depositions and uses that authority to reach the same result. Both sides acted at various times with and without substantial justification, so no sanctions are awarded.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, email:rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Ajdudication, By Defendants Geico General Insurance Company And Government Employees Insurance Company
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Seth Safier, Esq. (415-639-9090 x102) of Gutride Safier LLP for Plaintiff.
John Moura, Esq. (213-615-7035) of Freeman Mathis & Gary for Defendant.

RULING - ARGUED. Hearing is held. Parties report the deposition motion is uncontested. The Court explains the basis for the Motion for Summary Judgment. Defendant argues bad faith claim. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEES INSURANCE COMPANY, GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Ajdudication.

The court reaffirms the prior tentative ruling: Defendant GEICO General Insurance Company's motion for summary judgment is granted and defendant GEICO's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied in their entirety. General Insurance is not a party to the insurance policy and there is no evidentiary basis to impose liability on it. Plaintiff Sarah Leffert's request for a continuance per CCP 437c(h) is denied since she fails to show what evidence she may be able to obtain that would enable her to defeat General Insurance's motion for summary judgment. There are triable issues precluding summary judgment/adjudication in favor of GEICO on all three causes of action alleged against GEICO. Those triable issues include whether GEICO is actually providing uninsured motorist coverage to Ms. Leffert and whether GEICO acted unreasonably by initially deny uninsured motorist coverage. Ms. Leffert does not need to establish the elements cited in Hightower v. Farmers Ins. Exchange (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 853 to establish her bad faith claim. (Maslo v. Ameriprise Auto & Home Ins. (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 626, 638). (as fully reported in the reporter's transcritp)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, email:rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-30-2016 Set For Trial On Sep-11-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Kristen G. Simplicio, Esq. (415.639.9090) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Jennifer Sommer of the law firm of Gilbert Kelly Crowley & Jennett LLP (415.352.6416) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEES INSURANCE COMPANY AND GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as to all three causes of action in the complaint is OVERRULED as to all three causes of action. Plaintiff adequately alleges that defendants issued an insurance policy to her, breached the uninsured motorist coverage in the policy, and breached the covenant of good faith and fair dealing and violated the Unfair Competition Law by making misrepresentations that were designed to force plaintiff and others to pay a deductible when there is no contractual or other legal basis to require them to do so. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Kristen G. Simplicio, Esq. (415.639.9090) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Jennifer Sommer of the law firm of Gilbert Kelly Crowley & Jennett LLP (415.352.6416) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

Both sides submit on the tentative ruling. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEES INSURANCE COMPANY and GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, as to the punitive damages allegations in the complaint is DENIED. Plaintiff adequately alleges oppression and fraud to support entitlement to punitive damages by the allegations that defendants engage in a practice of making misrepresentations, which they did to plaintiff, to force their policyholders to pay a deductible when there no contractual or other legal basis to require them to do so. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
